concerns.text: T014a instructs to 'downscale (to high-resolution)' which contradicts FR-002 and US-1 requiring downscale to 'coarse spatial resolution' (30m/pixel). The task description is functionally inverted, failing to cover the requirement to simulate degradation.

FR-001 and Plan Phase 0 require downloading 1km² tiles and extracting 100m² patches for generation. T012 implements a loop for 500 samples but lacks a specific task to extract the 100m² patches from the downloaded 1km² tiles before degradation, creating a scope gap for the compute budget.

SC-003 requires per-sample logging of peak RAM and wall-clock time to verify feasibility. T026 only aggregates batch runtimes. The specific per-sample instrumentation logic defined in Plan T024 is missing from the tasks, risking inability to verify SC-003.

SC-002 requires identifying the critical NNF threshold where the p-value > 0.05. T032 mentions sweeping NNF and extracting a scalar but omits the explicit task to calculate the p-value at each step or perform the statistical test required to determine that specific threshold.

FR-006 requires validating synthetic masks against 'real cloud-masked images' by comparing statistical properties. T011b/T016 only mention acquiring a subset for 'tuning parameters' without a distinct task to perform the statistical comparison/validation against real data distributions.
Task ID T011b is a duplicate of the test task T011 (Unit test for degradation parameters). This creates a stale/ambiguous tag collision in the task list.
